message:
fix(btree): prevent is_table flag mismatch for index cursors

Three targeted fixes for B-tree corruption errors where index pages
were misidentified as table pages, causing "cell has no rowid" errors:

1. open_storage_cursor: Replace .unwrap_or((true, None)) fallback with
   raw page-type byte inspection. When header parsing fails, check the
   actual type byte (0x02/0x0A → index, 0x05/0x0D → table) instead of
   blindly defaulting to is_table=true. This prevents index cursors
   from being opened with wrong table semantics.

2. OpenAutoindex: Split from OpenEphemeral into its own handler.
   Autoindexes are INDEX B-trees (no rowid, key-only cells) but were
   being created as MemCursors where IdxInsert was a no-op. Now creates
   a proper StorageCursor backed by MemPageStore with is_table=false,
   enabling IdxInsert/IdxGE to work correctly on ephemeral autoindexes.

3. table_seek_for_insert: Add page-type mismatch guard. If a page's
   actual type is index but the cursor was opened with is_table=true,
   return a clear diagnostic error instead of the cryptic "table leaf
   cell has no rowid" message. Catches the bug regardless of root cause.

Addresses: beads_rust#138, #139, #140, #144, #145

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
